preview_text:
Iran emerges as the model's favorite with a 48% win probability, while Egypt's chances stand at 23%. The gap reflects a meaningful edge in the statistical forecast, though it stops well short of a dominant prediction.

The draw probability of 30% suggests this match carries genuine competitive balance despite Iran's higher win likelihood. The trio of outcomes—Iran 48%, draw 30%, Egypt 23%—spreads across the probability space without one outcome heavily suppressing the others. A stalemate remains a plausible result, indicating the teams are not vastly separated in expected strength.

No sportsbook consensus is available yet. The model's probabilities can be tracked against market prices once they emerge; early discrepancies, if they appear, will be visible on the dashboard as odds stabilize.
